entries.source: Chat Edit: 'all three in parallel immediately?'

Chat Edit: ' Security (FIPS/HSM/Sovereign)
FIPS: Stub path logs a one-time warning that the default build uses an XOR stub and is not FIPS compliant; use RAWR_HAS_FIPS for production.
HSM: Stub path logs that PKCS#11 is not linked and no real HSM calls are made; use -DRAWR_HAS_PKCS11 and link the PKCS#11 library for production.
Sovereign key mgmt: Constructor and encrypt path document that the default build uses an XOR/static-key stub and is not for production.
2. CUDA inference engine
File header states the implementation is 100% MOCK (no cudaMalloc/cudaSetDevice/GPU kernels).
initializeCUDA() already prints: [CUDA] MOCK mode: no GPU calls. Real CUDA requires ggml-cuda or CUDA toolkit build.
3. 21 enterprise-licensed features with no implementation
Gate: In enterprise_licensev2_impl.cpp, gate(FeatureID id, const char* caller) now checks isFeatureImplemented(id) first. If the feature is not implemented, it calls recordAudit(id, false, caller, "Feature not implemented") and returns false, so unimplemented features are denied and audited.
Doc: ENTERPRISE_FEATURES_NO_IMPLEMENTATION.md lists all 20 manifest entries with implemented: false (CUDABackend, HIPBackend, FlashAttention, SpeculativeDecoding, ContinuousBatching, GPTQ/AWQ, PriorityQueuing, RateLimitingEngine, RBAC, AirGappedDeploy, HSM, FIPS140_2, CustomSecurityPolicies, SovereignKeyMgmt, ClassifiedNetwork, ImmutableAuditLogs, KubernetesSupport, TamperDetection, SecureBootChain), with tier, notes, and pointers to gate(), manifest, and security stubs.
All three areas are covered: security stubs are clearly labeled, CUDA is documented and logged as mock-only, and unimplemented enterprise features are denied at the gate with an audit reason and documented in one place.'
